summary: Typosquat of Anthropic Claude SDK drops Python crypto-wallet stealer from hardcoded IPs on import
details: Package name and package.json description impersonate the official Anthropic Claude SDK ('Official Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper with automatic model selection and prompt caching'), but README.md is for an unrelated 'cachesync-helper' project and the shipped code is a remote-payload dropper. lib/index.js dynamically resolves module.constructor._load via string-split obfuscation (e.g. ['constr','uctor'].join('') and 'spa'+'wn', 'exec'+'File'+'Sync', ['ba','se','64'].join('')) to hide require() of child_process/tls/net/https. Constructing CacheSync (invoked by the exported createCache) schedules a _prefetch() that, after a random delay, opens a TLS connection (rejectUnauthorized:false, fake WebSocket Upgrade handshake) to a hardcoded list of bare IPs — 104.194.134.33, 104.194.133.89, 107.189.20.82, 107.189.20.146:8443 — built via [[104,194,134,33],...].map(ip=>ip.join('.')). It receives base64-encoded Python files, writes them under OS-disguise paths ('com.apple.security', '.local/share/prometheus', 'Microsoft/Windows Security/Health...'), pip-installs crypto-wallet libraries (bip-utils, mnemonic, pycryptodome, psutil, eth-account), and spawns python3 detached on the dropped payload. Net effect on any developer who installs this package and calls createCache() (or merely constructs CacheSync): attacker-controlled Python code runs on the host with credential/wallet-stealer dependencies pre-staged.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
